original_image_tags: A scanned image of the front of a white, rectangular, vintage envelope. The paper shows age with slight yellowing and faint brown staining in the lower-left quadrant. The top-left corner is torn and missing. The envelope is addressed via typewriter. In the upper-right corner are two identical purple United States postage stamps, each 3 cents, depicting the Statue of Liberty. They are cancelled by a circular postmark. Handwritten annotations in blue ink and pencil are visible.

elements.description: A scan of the front of a white envelope. Top left return address: 'VENTURE PRESS', '305 STUDIO BUILDING', '1718 SHERMAN AVENUE', 'EVANSTON, ILLINOIS'. Center typed address: 'Mr. Kenneth Arnold,', 'Box 387', 'Boise, Idaho'. Top right: two purple 3-cent US postage stamps (showing a profile portrait) with a circular postmark reading 'CHICAGO ILL', 'AUG 5', '4 30 PM', '1947', '22'. Large handwritten pencil text in center: '62-83894-111'. Handwritten blue ink text below postmark: 'AIR MAIL' with a line underneath.

judge.reason: The descriptions contradict each other on the core identity of the stamps: one states they depict the Statue of Liberty, while the other states they show a profile portrait, and they provide different, incompatible postmark details.
